slides.title: From Words to Agents
slides.notes: Title slide -- frame the journey from basic text to intelligent agents.

children.content: How LLMs Think, Speak, and Act
A visual journey from tokens to autonomous agents

slides.title: Tokenization
slides.notes: BPE tokenization: text -> subword tokens -> integer IDs. ~50k vocabulary for GPT models.

children.content: Breaking text into pieces the model can understand
BPE (Byte Pair Encoding) maps subwords to integer IDs
Common words = 1 token  |  Rare words = multiple tokens

slides.title: Embeddings
slides.notes: Token IDs -> dense vectors via learned embedding table. Positional encoding added for sequence order.

children.content: Each token ID becomes a high-dimensional vector
Adds position information so the model knows word order

slides.title: Attention
slides.notes: Self-attention: Q*K^T/sqrt(d_k) computes relevance scores, softmax normalizes, multiply by V for weighted output.

children.content: How tokens learn to look at each other
Each head learns different relationships (syntax, semantics, position)

slides.title: Transformer Block
slides.notes: Transformer block: Multi-Head Attention -> Add&Norm -> FFN -> Add&Norm. Residual connections prevent vanishing gradients. Repeated 12-96 times.

children.content: This block is repeated N times (N = 12 to 96+ layers)

slides.title: Deep Representations
slides.notes: Stacked transformer layers build increasingly abstract representations. Early layers = syntax, deep layers = reasoning.

children.content: Each layer has millions of learnable weights

slides.title: Next Token Prediction
slides.notes: Final layer output -> linear projection -> softmax = probability distribution over ~50K tokens. Sampling strategy selects the next token.

children.content: The output is a probability distribution over the entire vocabulary
Temperature controls randomness: T=0 -> greedy, T=1 -> diverse

slides.title: Autoregressive Loop
slides.notes: Autoregressive = generate one token at a time, append to context, repeat. Stops at EOS or max length. KV-cache avoids redundant computation.

children.content: Generate one token at a time, feeding output back as input
<EOS> = End of Sequence token signals completion
The entire context is re-processed each step (KV-cache optimizes this)

slides.title: Structured Output
slides.notes: LLMs trained with tool schemas learn to output structured JSON tool calls instead of free text. Same autoregressive mechanism.

children.content: LLMs can be trained to emit structured JSON, including tool calls
Same model, same mechanism -- the training data teaches the format
The model sees tool definitions and learns when to call them
Formats: OpenAI function_call | Anthropic tool_use | Open-source ReAct

slides.title: The Agentic Loop
slides.notes: The agentic loop: User query -> LLM reasons -> emits tool call -> tool executes -> result appended to thread -> LLM reasons again. Repeats until done.

children.content: Each iteration: full transformer forward pass on the growing thread

slides.title: The Complete Pipeline
slides.notes: Complete pipeline: text -> BPE -> embed -> Nx transformer layers -> softmax -> sample. Tool call patterns trigger the agentic loop. Each iteration = full forward pass.

children.content: When the model predicts a tool call token pattern:
The entire autoregressive pipeline runs inside each agentic iteration

slides.title: Thank You
slides.notes: Closing slide -- recap the journey from tokens to agents. Each concept builds on the previous.

children.content: Every intelligent agent action begins with predicting the next token
Attention: tokens learn to look at each other
Transformer: attention + FFN, stacked N times
Autoregressive: generate one token at a time
Tool calls: structured output from same mechanism
Agentic loop: reason -> act -> observe -> repeat
Built with Elucim -- animated explanations for the web
